message:
send: add --base-prompt and --base-prompt-file flags (bd-3ejl)

Prepend common instructions to all send prompts. Supports inline
string, file path, and config (send.base_prompt / send.base_prompt_file).
Priority: flag > flag-file > config-string > config-file. Works with
regular sends, batch mode, and template-based sends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -672,7 +672,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3dxk","title":"EPIC: End-of-Session Output Summarization","description":"**IDEA #13 - PROGRESS CLARITY (72% confidence)**

## What It Does
When ending a session, automatically generate a summary of what each agent accomplished, decisions made, and work remaining.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Users must mentally track what each agent did
- After: Clear record of session accomplishments
- Valuable for handoffs, standup reports, documentation

## Commands
ntm summarize myproject
ntm kill myproject --summarize

## Summary Generation Process
1. Capture full scrollback from all panes
2. Extract accomplishments using pattern matching
3. Extract key decisions
4. Integrate with git diff for concrete changes

## Technical Implementation
- GenerateSessionSummary() analyzing pane output
- extractAccomplishments() using pattern matching
- extractDecisions() identifying key choices
- Integration with git diff for concrete changes

## Dependencies
Depends on: Output Archive (for reliable capture)

## Success Criteria

1. ntm summarize generates coherent session summary
2. ntm kill --summarize works at session end
3. Accomplishments extracted from agent output
4. Decisions identified and logged
5. Git diffs included in summary
6. JSON output in robot mode
7. Unit tests for extraction logic (80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify summary generation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:27:30.387608415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:42.179031130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:42.178954267Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dxk","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":101,"issue_id":"bd-3dxk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added git diff/state integration to summaries by wiring ProjectDir+IncludeGitDiff in CLI summary and end-of-session monitor; added TestSummarizeSessionIncludesGitChanges. go test ./internal/summary passes.","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:10:58Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ef1l","title":"Task: WebSocket streaming tests","description":"## Scope\nAutomated tests for WebSocket streaming, resume, and backpressure.\n\n## Logging Requirements\n- Log subscription topics, seq numbers, and dropped counts.\n- Log reconnect attempts and cursor resets.\n\n## Tests\n- Subscribe to pane output and verify sequence continuity.\n- Disconnect/reconnect with since cursor.\n- Force backpressure and verify pane.output.dropped event.\n- Multi‑client fan‑out correctness.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- WS tests pass deterministically.\n- Resume and backpressure behaviors validated.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-opus-4.5","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:55:08.244291999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:23.246102626Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:23.245965070Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-cs5x4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-excs1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3ejl","title":"Add ntm send --base-prompt flag - prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically","description":"## Overview
Add --base-prompt flag to prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically.

## Problem
Users often want to include standard instructions (coding standards, project context, safety rules) with every prompt but don't want to repeat them.

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Always run tests after changes" --msg "Implement feature X"`

The actual prompt sent becomes:
```
Always run tests after changes

Implement feature X
```

## Configuration
Can also be set in ntm.yaml:
```yaml
send:
  base_prompt: |
    Follow AGENTS.md rules.
    Run ubs before committing.
    Use br to track work.
```

## Implementation
```go
func buildPrompt(basePrompt, userPrompt string) string {
    if basePrompt == "" {
        return userPrompt
    }
    return basePrompt + "\n\n" + userPrompt
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Inline base prompt
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Run tests" --msg "Add feature"

# From file
ntm send myproject --base-prompt-file context.md --msg "Add feature"

# With batch (prepended to each)
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Be careful" --batch prompts.txt
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Base prompt prepended to all messages
- [ ] Works with --msg and --batch
- [ ] Config file support
- [ ] File-based base prompt (--base-prompt-file)
- [ ] Empty base prompt = no-op
- [ ] Clear separator between base and user prompt

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Prompt concatenation
- Unit test: Config file loading
- E2E test: Base prompt with batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

(1) Added SendConfig struct to config with base_prompt and base_prompt_file fields. (2) Added resolveBasePrompt function with priority: flag > file flag > config string > config file. (3) Added applyBasePrompt function that prepends base prompt with blank-line separator. (4) Applied base prompt in both regular send (runSendInternal) and batch send (runSendBatch). (5) Also works with template-based sends via runSendWithTemplate. (6) 12 unit tests: ApplyBasePrompt (5 subtests), ResolveBasePrompt priority/file/config/empty/missing-file/missing-config-file. All tests pass, build clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ejl","depends_on_id":"bd-10ufs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ejl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kmf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3eu7h","title":"Unit Tests: Robot Env Info","description":"Unit tests for Robot Env Info command.

## Test Coverage

### tmux Detection Tests
- Test binary path detection (/usr/bin/tmux vs alias)
- Test version parsing from various formats
- Test shell alias detection (zsh, bash)
- Test plugin detection (oh-my-zsh tmux plugin)

### Session Structure Tests
- Test window index detection
- Test pane index range parsing
- Test control vs agent pane identification
- Test empty session handling

### Shell Environment Tests
- Test shell type detection (zsh, bash, fish)
- Test oh-my-zsh detection
- Test config path discovery
- Test plugin enumeration

### Timing Tests
- Test default values returned
- Test config file override
- Test environment variable override
- Test priority order (config > env > default)

### Targeting Tests
- Test example generation correctness
- Test format string accuracy
- Test various session names (with special chars)

## Mocking Strategy

- Mock which/type commands
- Mock tmux list-* output
- Mock file system reads for config detection
- Mock environment variables

## Logging Requirements

Each test must log:
- Mock configuration at test start
- Command outputs being simulated
- Expected vs actual values
- Full response object on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:30:00.562685465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:25:22.665394075Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:25:22.665290850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-1ws17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-28n0d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-2wkrv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-35xyt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-cqnm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3exkg","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Probe","description":"E2E tests for Robot Probe with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Responsive Agent (keystroke_echo)
Spawn session, wait for agent initialization, execute probe, verify responsive=true

### Scenario 2: Unresponsive Pane (shell prompt)
Create session with just shell (no agent), execute probe, verify shell responds

### Scenario 3: Hung Process Simulation
Spawn session, make pane unresponsive with infinite loop, probe should detect unresponsive

### Scenario 4: Method Comparison
Test keystroke_echo vs interrupt_test methods, compare results

### Scenario 5: Custom Timeout
Test short (100ms) and long (10s) timeouts, verify behavior changes

### Scenario 6: Aggressive Mode Fallback
Test aggressive mode falls back to interrupt_test if keystroke_echo fails

### Scenario 7: Invalid Pane
Test with pane 999, verify PANE_NOT_FOUND error

### Scenario 8: Integration with Diagnose
Verify probe and diagnose results are consistent

## Logging Requirements
Each test logs: scenario name, setup params, probe params, result JSON, assertions, pass/fail, duration_ms

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 8 test scenarios implemented as bash scripts
2. Each scenario logs full JSON responses
3. Assertions verify specific JSON fields
4. Cleanup removes all test sessions
5. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
6. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure
7. Total test suite runs in under 5 minutes","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 8 test scenarios pass
2. Test logs follow JSON format specification
3. Cleanup removes all test sessions
4. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
5. Test duration under 5 minutes total
6. Each scenario logs setup/execute/verify/result
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure
8. Test runner script executable: scripts/e2e_robot_probe.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:45.110817161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:44:02.389055346Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:44:02.388981058Z","close_reason":"E2E probe script already present and executable","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3exkg","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3exkg","depends_on_id":"bd-43oxm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
